title: Relay Pumping: LDH Supply to Attack Engine
inputLabel: Your relay pump discharge pressure answer
question: Engine 181 is at the hydrant supplying Engine 183 through 800 feet of 4" LDH. Engine 183 needs 500 GPM delivered. The relay lay has a 30-foot elevation gain from Engine 181 to Engine 183. Engine 183 needs 20 PSI residual intake pressure. What discharge pressure should Engine 181 pump?
formulaBreakdown: Relay Pumping PDP = friction loss + elevation pressure + desired residual intake pressure.
FL = C × (GPM / 100)^2 × hose length in hundreds of feet.
Desired residual intake pressure at Engine 183 = 20 PSI.
Nozzle pressure is 0 because Engine 181 is supplying the receiving engine, not the nozzle.
instructorExplanation: This is a relay supply problem. Engine 181 only needs to overcome LDH friction loss, elevation gain, and maintain the desired intake pressure at Engine 183.
explainMistake: Common mistakes: adding nozzle pressure, forgetting residual intake pressure, forgetting elevation gain, using the wrong C-value for 4" LDH, or calculating friction loss for only 100 feet instead of 800 feet.

variations.change: Change hose length from 800' to 1000'. Same 500 GPM, +30' elevation, and 20 PSI desired intake pressure.
Change flow from 500 GPM to 600 GPM. Same 800' of 4" LDH, +30' elevation, and 20 PSI desired intake pressure.
Change elevation gain from 30' to 50'. Same 800' of 4" LDH, 500 GPM, and 20 PSI desired intake pressure.
